summary: chai-presentation performs import-time remote code execution via jsonkeeper.com paste
details: On require of chai-presentation, index.js spawns a detached, stdio-ignored Node child process to execute lib/caller.js. That file issues an HTTP GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PC5CK and passes the response body into `new Function('require',...)` invoked with the host's require, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript on the installer's machine with full Node privileges. A secondary code-delivery path builds a URL from configuration and, on an HTTP 404 whose body contains a `token` field, passes that body into `Function.constructor('require', res.token)` and invokes it — a covert second-stage loader hiding code delivery inside an error response. The detached+unref+stdio-ignore shape is deliberate concealment: the payload survives parent exit and produces no visible output. The package advertises itself as a Chai assertion plugin (validJWT, safeString) using a name that trades on the popular chai library, providing cover to be pulled into test/CI environments where its load-time RCE fires on any `require('chai-presentation')`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
